supporting_evidence: During collapse, render probes show animating=true while revealProgress interpolates from 0.904 down to 0.009, but detailFrameHeightParam remains 0.000 and detailVisibleHeight remains 0.000 for every sampled frame.

Opacity probe samples show interpolatedOpacity animating smoothly (0.853 -> 0.009) while render-path detailOpacity stays 0.000, indicating interpolation exists but the rendered branch is already in collapsed target state.
contradicting_evidence: No evidence in this incident of tentative-vs-committed state channel divergence; the failure is localized to row render interpolation under active animation.
rowToken is stable (FCA096BA-98E6-4482-8C03-D4579EFD101D) across all captured samples, which argues against row/view identity recreation as primary cause.
missing_evidence: Earliest probe samples (1-3) are absent in this incident payload due truncation, so the exact first-frame snap boundary is inferred from previous incident evidence.
candidates.next_discriminating_check: Inspect the detail render code path to verify whether frame/clip parameters are driven by a discrete boolean (isExpanded) instead of an animatable scalar during collapse.
